skill.name: dbt-transformation-patterns
skill.description: Master dbt (data build tool) for analytics engineering with model organization, testing, documentation, and incremental strategies.
skill.summary: Production-ready patterns for building data transformation pipelines with dbt including model layers, testing strategies, and documentation.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: This skill is safe for publication. Static analysis detected 70 patterns that are all false positives - the flagged content consists of SQL/YAML code examples in markdown documentation, not executable code. The skill provides reference patterns for dbt (data build tool) analytics engineering workflows.

content.user_title: Build production-ready dbt data transformation pipelines
content.value_statement: Analytics teams struggle with inconsistent data models and poor documentation. This skill provides proven patterns for organizing dbt projects with proper testing, documentation, and incremental processing.
actual_capabilities: Structure dbt projects using staging, intermediate, and marts model layers
Implement incremental materializations for large datasets
Create comprehensive data quality tests and documentation
Define source freshness monitoring and data contracts
Build reusable macros for DRY SQL code
limitations: Requires existing dbt project setup and data warehouse access
Examples focus on Stripe and Shopify sources - adapt for your data sources
Does not cover dbt Cloud-specific features or CI/CD pipelines
Incremental strategies may need adjustment for your warehouse type

use_cases.title: Analytics engineer building transformation pipelines
use_cases.description: Set up a new dbt project with proper model organization, testing, and documentation from day one.

use_cases.title: Data team improving model quality
use_cases.description: Add comprehensive tests, documentation, and freshness monitoring to existing dbt models.

use_cases.title: Engineering team optimizing incremental loads
use_cases.description: Implement efficient incremental strategies for large fact tables to reduce compute costs.

prompt_templates.title: Create a staging model
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a dbt staging model for the [source_name] [table_name] table. Include source definition with column descriptions, a staging SQL model with proper renaming conventions, and basic tests for primary key and not-null constraints.
prompt_templates.scenario: You have a new raw table and need to create the first model in your dbt project.

prompt_templates.title: Build an incremental fact table
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a dbt incremental fact table model named fct_[name] that tracks [business_process]. Configure it for incremental materialization with [strategy], define the unique_key, and include the incremental filter logic using is_incremental().
prompt_templates.scenario: You need to build a large fact table that should incrementally update rather than full refresh.

prompt_templates.title: Add tests and documentation
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this dbt model SQL and generate the corresponding YAML documentation file. Include model description, column descriptions, and appropriate tests (unique, not_null, relationships, accepted_values) based on the columns present.
prompt_templates.scenario: You have existing models that lack documentation and tests.

prompt_templates.title: Design a complete model layer
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a dbt model structure for a [domain] analytics workflow. Define the staging models needed for [sources], intermediate models for business logic, and mart models (dimensions and facts) for final output. Include naming conventions and materialization strategies for each layer.
prompt_templates.scenario: You are starting a new analytics domain and need to plan the complete model architecture.

output_examples.input: Create a staging model for Stripe payments source
output: Source definition YAML with payments table schema and tests
Column renaming following naming conventions (id to payment_id, amount in cents to dollars)
Incremental configuration with unique_key and updated_at filter

output_examples.input: Build a customer dimension with payment metrics
output: dim_customers.sql joining customer and payment intermediate models
Customer tier segmentation logic based on lifetime value
YAML documentation with column descriptions and tests

best_practices: Use staging layer for all source data - clean once, reuse everywhere
Test aggressively with not_null, unique, and relationship tests on all keys
Document every model and column with business context for downstream users
anti_patterns: Skipping the staging layer and querying sources directly in marts creates technical debt
Hardcoding dates or values instead of using dbt variables for configuration
Repeating business logic across models instead of extracting to reusable macros

faq.question: What is the medallion architecture in dbt?
faq.answer: The medallion architecture organizes models into layers: staging (raw data cleaning), intermediate (business logic), and marts (final analytics tables). This separation ensures clean data flows consistently through your pipeline.

faq.question: When should I use incremental materialization?
faq.answer: Use incremental models for tables with more than 1M rows or when source data grows continuously. Incremental models only process new or changed records, reducing compute time and costs significantly.

faq.question: What tests should I add to my models?
faq.answer: Add not_null and unique tests on all primary keys, relationship tests on foreign keys, not_null on required columns, and accepted_values on status or type columns. Use dbt_utils.expression_is_true for custom validation.

faq.question: How do I choose between delete+insert and merge incremental strategies?
faq.answer: Use delete+insert for most warehouses as the default. Use merge when you need to update existing records with late-arriving data. Use insert_overwrite for partition-based workflows on BigQuery or similar.

faq.question: What is the purpose of ephemeral models?
faq.answer: Ephemeral models are intermediate CTEs that do not materialize as tables. Use them for reusable logic that is always referenced by other models, reducing the number of tables in your warehouse.

faq.question: How do I document my dbt project effectively?
faq.answer: Add descriptions to every model and column in YAML files. Use the description field to explain business logic, data sources, and expected values. Generate dbt docs and serve them for your team to browse.
